rationale: Keep: the citing paper explicitly positions its contribution against “classic partitioned pricing” and frames its research gap around prior partitioned/add-on pricing evidence, which aligns directly with the cited paper’s meta-analysis on when partitioned pricing works. Given the cited study’s broad synthesis of moderators (e.g., total-price presence) and the strong topical overlap, this is plausibly a substantive theory/background anchor rather than an incidental mention.
